seo.title: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Semantic Kernel Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix responsible ai governance for agents in Semantic Kernel agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: Responsible AI Governance for Agents in Semantic Kernel manifests as uncontrolled plugin chaining, invalid planner outputs, hallucinated memory recalls, and cross-plugin data leaks that violate enterprise compliance. These issues arise from Semantic Kernel's flexible plugin architecture, Handlebars planners, and volatile memory stores, creating cascading failures that expose sensitive data and produce unreliable agent behaviors. Without governance, agents can execute harmful actions or propagate errors across enterprise workflows.

howItManifests.symptom: Sequential plugin calls where one failure corrupts downstream inputs
howItManifests.impact: Complete workflow failure and data corruption across business processes

howItManifests.symptom: Handlebars planner creates non-existent function sequences
howItManifests.impact: Agent stuck in infinite loops or crashes during execution
Agents make decisions based on nonexistent user interactions

howItManifests.symptom: Customer data from Finance plugin leaks into HR plugin context
howItManifests.impact: Regulatory violations (GDPR, HIPAA) and security breaches

detection.name: Plugin Chain Error Cascade Detector
detection.description: Monitors sequential plugin invocations for error propagation patterns

detection.name: Invalid Planner Plan Validator
detection.description: Validates Handlebars planner output against registered functions

detection.name: Memory Hallucination Checker
detection.description: Cross-references memory store with chat history for consistency

fixes.name: Circuit Breaker Plugin Wrapper
fixes.description: Stops plugin chains after 2 consecutive errors

fixes.name: Function Registry Planner Guard
fixes.description: Validates plans against function registry before execution

fixes.name: Plugin Context Isolation
fixes.description: Prevents cross-plugin data contamination with isolated contexts

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Responsible AI Governance for Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a Semantic Kernel filter on function results, automatically detecting the four common failure modes in real-time. It monitors plugin invocation chains, validates planner outputs against the function registry, cross-checks memory recalls for hallucination, and enforces data isolation boundaries across plugins. Vex provides enterprise-grade observability with automatic remediation workflows that inject circuit breakers, validate plans before execution, and quarantine contaminated contexts.

The Vex SDK (`pip install vex-sdk`) adds zero-boilerplate integration through kernel filters, capturing all agent interactions without modifying core business logic. Vex's ML-powered anomaly detection identifies cascading failures before they impact production workflows, while its governance engine enforces compliance policies across multi-agent systems. Remediation is automatic: Vex can rewrite invalid plans, isolate contaminated data flows, and rollback hallucinated memory states.

Vex dashboards provide drill-down analysis showing exactly which plugins caused failures, what data was contaminated, and recommended fixes with one-click deployment. This shifts Responsible AI from manual audits to continuous, automated governance across all Semantic Kernel deployments.
vexAutomation.code:
```python
from semantic_kernel import Kernel
import vex

# Install: pip install semantic-kernel vex-sdk

kernel = Kernel()

# Add Vex as kernel filter on ALL function results
@vex.guardian(
    detect=["plugin_chain_cascade", "planner_invalid", "memory_hallucination", "data_contamination"],
    remediate=True,
    policy="enterprise_ra_governance"
)
def kernel_filter(result):
    """Vex automatically governs all kernel function outputs"""
    return result

# Apply globally
kernel.register_function_filter(kernel_filter)

# Vex now auto-detects and fixes all 4 failure modes
agent = kernel.create_agent()
response = agent.invoke('complex multi-plugin workflow')

# Vex dashboard shows: 2/10 chains blocked, 1 hallucination fixed
print(vex.metrics())
```
